chosen.content: QLoRA reduces fine-tuning memory by loading the frozen base model in 4-bit precision and training LoRA adapters on top.

rejected.content: QLoRA is just a faster optimizer and does not change memory usage.

prompt.content: Why keep adapter checkpoints separate at first?

chosen.content: Keeping adapters separate makes experiments easier to compare, store, and merge only when deployment requires a standalone model.

rejected.content: Adapters should always be merged immediately because separate checkpoints cannot be evaluated.
